document.title: Installation
document.description: Get Prizmux set up in your React Native project

body.raw:
# Installation

Get Prizmux installed and ready to use in your project with just a few steps.

## Prerequisites

- React Native 0.60 or higher
- React 16.8 or higher
- Node.js 14+

## Install via npm

```bash
npm install prizmux
```

## Install via yarn

```bash
yarn add prizmux
```

## Install via pnpm

```bash
pnpm add prizmux
```

## Basic Setup

Once installed, you can start using components immediately:

```tsx
import { Button } from 'prizmux'

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Button 
      title="Press Me" 
      onPress={() => console.log('Pressed!')}
    />
  )
}
```

## TypeScript Support

Prizmux comes with full TypeScript support. All components are fully typed with JSDoc comments for better IDE autocomplete:

```tsx
import { Button, ButtonProps } from 'prizmux'

const handlePress: ButtonProps['onPress'] = () => {
  console.log('Button pressed')
}

export default function App() {
  return <Button title="Press Me" onPress={handlePress} />
}
```

## Peer Dependencies

Prizmux requires very few peer dependencies:

- `react-native` - The React Native framework
- `react` - React library
- `react-native-gesture-handler` - For gesture support (optional, but recommended)

That's it! No heavy dependencies to worry about.

## Troubleshooting

### Component not found
Make sure you're importing from the main `prizmux` package:

```tsx
// ✅ Correct
import { Button } from 'prizmux'

// ❌ Wrong
import { Button } from 'prizmux/lib/components'
```

### TypeScript errors
Ensure your `tsconfig.json` includes:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "strict": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true
  }
}
```

### Still having issues?
Check out the [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/prizmux/prizmux/issues) or open a new discussion on our [Community](/docs/community).

## Next Steps

- Explore [Components](/docs/components) to see what's available
- Read the [Philosophy](/docs/philosophy) behind Prizmux
- Check out [Examples](/docs/examples) for real-world usage patterns

document.title: Introduction
document.description: Learn about Prizmux and why you should use it

body.raw:
# Introduction to Prizmux

Prizmux is a **developer-first React Native component system** designed for production applications. It provides a collection of battle-tested, fully-typed components that focus on **zero unnecessary dependencies** and maximum flexibility.

## Why Prizmux?

Building production-grade React Native applications often means dealing with complex component libraries that bring along heavy dependencies and inflexible designs. Prizmux takes a different approach:

### Zero Unnecessary Dependencies
Every component in Prizmux is built with minimal dependencies. We believe in shipping less JavaScript, which means faster apps and fewer version conflicts.

### Fully Typed
Complete TypeScript support means better IDE autocomplete, fewer runtime errors, and improved developer experience. All props are fully documented.

### Bring Your Own Icons
Don't like our icon choice? No problem. Prizmux lets you bring your own icon library or use whatever works best for your design system.

### Production Ready
Used in production by developers building real applications. Components handle edge cases, support accessibility, and work across different React Native platforms.

## Component Library

Prizmux includes a collection of essential components:

- **Button** — Flexible button component with variants and sizes
- **Card** — Composable card for content grouping
- **Bottom Sheet** — Smooth bottom sheet modal
- **Image Preview** — Image gallery with touch gestures
- **Header with Back** — Navigation header with back button
- **Empty State** — Placeholder for empty data states
- **Phone Input** — Formatted phone number input

## Philosophy

We believe component libraries should:

1. **Be lightweight** — Dependencies add bloat and maintenance burden
2. **Be flexible** — Adapt to your design, not the other way around
3. **Be explicit** — Clear, predictable APIs without hidden magic
4. **Be accessible** — Support screen readers and keyboard navigation
5. **Be typed** — TypeScript support out of the box

## Who Should Use Prizmux?

Prizmux is perfect for:
- Production React Native applications
- Teams building design systems
- Projects that value minimal dependencies
- Developers who want full control over their components
- Applications targeting multiple platforms (iOS, Android, Web)

## Getting Started

Ready to explore? Head over to the [Installation](/docs/installation) guide to get started, or jump straight into a [Component](/docs/components) to see what Prizmux can do.

document.title: Philosophy
document.description: The principles behind Prizmux component design

body.raw:
# Philosophy

Prizmux was built on a set of core principles that guide every design decision. Understanding this philosophy will help you get the most out of the library.

## 1. Minimal Dependencies

Every dependency added to a library becomes a transitive dependency for your application. We believe in being extremely conservative about this.

Each component in Prizmux is designed to avoid unnecessary packages. When external packages are needed, they're carefully selected for maturity and minimal surface area.

**Result**: Smaller bundle sizes, fewer version conflicts, and less maintenance overhead for your project.

## 2. Explicit Over Implicit

We prefer clear, explicit APIs over hidden magic. This means:

- Props are fully typed and well-documented
- No global configuration that affects component behavior
- Predictable component behavior
- Easy to debug when something doesn't work as expected

```tsx
// Explicit - you know exactly what's happening
<Button 
  title="Submit" 
  variant="primary"
  size="lg"
  onPress={handleSubmit}
/>
```

## 3. Composition Over Inheritance

Components are designed to be composed together, not extended. This makes it easy to build complex UIs by combining simple, focused components.

```tsx
// ✅ Compose components together
<Card>
  <Header title="Profile" />
  <Content>
    <Avatar />
    <Text>John Doe</Text>
  </Content>
</Card>

// ❌ Don't extend components
class ExtendedCard extends Card {
  // ...
}
```

## 4. Accessibility First

Every component is built with accessibility in mind from day one:

- Keyboard navigation support
- Screen reader compatibility
- High contrast mode support
- ARIA labels and roles where applicable

Accessibility isn't an afterthought—it's part of the design.

## 5. Flexibility in Design

We don't force a specific design system on you. Components are:

- Styleable with your own theme
- Compatible with custom icons
- Adaptable to different platforms
- Customizable through slots and render props

You bring the design, we provide the component logic.

## 6. Production Ready

Prizmux components are battle-tested in production applications:

- Handle edge cases gracefully
- Perform well with large datasets
- Support loading and error states
- Built with performance in mind

## Design Decisions

### Why no styled-components?
We avoid CSS-in-JS libraries to reduce bundle size and improve consistency with React Native's native styling.

### Why TypeScript only?
We believe type safety improves developer experience and catches bugs early. The entire library is written in TypeScript.

### Why bring your own icons?
Icon libraries are a significant source of bloat. We let you choose the icon library that works best for your project.

### Why minimal theming?
Heavy theming systems add complexity. Prizmux provides simple theming through React context, letting you build on top of it if needed.

---

These principles ensure Prizmux remains lightweight, maintainable, and developer-friendly.

document.title: Bottom Sheet
document.description: A smooth modal that slides up from the bottom

document.title: Button
document.description: A versatile button component for all your interactive needs

body.raw:
# Button

The Button component is the foundation of user interactions in your app. It supports multiple variants, sizes, and states out of the box.

## Overview

Button is a flexible component that handles touch interactions, loading states, and disabled states. It's fully typed and accessible by default.

**Use it for:**
- Primary actions in your UI
- Form submissions
- Navigation triggers
- Any interactive element requiring user action

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `title` | `string` | - | The button label text |
| `onPress` | `() => void` | - | Callback when button is pressed |
| `variant` | `'primary' \| 'secondary' \| 'outline'` | `'primary'` | Visual style variant |
| `size` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Button size |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disable user interactions |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Show loading state |
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Icon to display |
| `iconPosition` | `'left' \| 'right'` | `'left'` | Icon placement |

## Usage

### Basic Button

```tsx
import { Button } from 'prizmux'

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Button 
      title="Press Me" 
      onPress={() => alert('Button pressed!')}
    />
  )
}
```

### Variants

```tsx
<Button title="Primary" variant="primary" />
<Button title="Secondary" variant="secondary" />
<Button title="Outline" variant="outline" />
```

### Sizes

```tsx
<Button title="Small" size="sm" />
<Button title="Medium" size="md" />
<Button title="Large" size="lg" />
```

### With Icon

```tsx
import { Button } from 'prizmux'
import { ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react-native'

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Button 
      title="Next" 
      icon={<ChevronRight size={18} />}
      iconPosition="right"
    />
  )
}
```

### Loading State

```tsx
const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)

const handleSubmit = async () => {
  setLoading(true)
  await submitForm()
  setLoading(false)
}

<Button 
  title="Submit" 
  loading={loading}
  onPress={handleSubmit}
/>
```

## Notes

- The button automatically handles opacity changes on press
- Loading state disables user interaction automatically
- Accessibility features are built-in (press states, labels)
- Works with custom icon libraries (Lucide, Font Awesome, etc.)
- Full TypeScript support with IntelliSense

document.title: Card
document.description: A composable container for grouping related content

body.raw:
# Card

Card is a flexible container component that provides a consistent surface for grouping related content. It's designed for maximum composability.

## Overview

Card acts as a container with built-in styling for borders, shadows, and spacing. Combine it with other components to create complex layouts.

**Use it for:**
- Content containers
- Information cards
- Product listings
- Settings panels
- Data summaries

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Card content |
| `variant` | `'filled' \| 'outline'` | `'filled'` | Visual style |
| `padding` | `'sm' \| 'md' \| 'lg'` | `'md'` | Internal spacing |
| `onPress` | `() => void` | - | Make card pressable |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disable interactions |

## Usage

### Basic Card

```tsx
import { Card, Text } from 'prizmux'

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Card>
      <Text>Card content goes here</Text>
    </Card>
  )
}
```

### Outline Variant

```tsx
<Card variant="outline">
  <Text>This card has a border</Text>
</Card>
```

### Pressable Card

```tsx
const [selected, setSelected] = useState(false)

<Card 
  onPress={() => setSelected(!selected)}
  style={{ borderWidth: selected ? 2 : 0 }}
>
  <Text>Tap me to select</Text>
</Card>
```

### Complex Layout

```tsx
import { Card, Text, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { Share2 } from 'lucide-react-native'

export default function ProductCard() {
  return (
    <Card padding="lg">
      <Text variant="title">Product Name</Text>
      <Text variant="caption">$99.99</Text>
      
      <View style={{ gap: 8, marginTop: 16 }}>
        <Text numberOfLines={2}>
          Product description goes here
        </Text>
      </View>
      
      <View style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, marginTop: 16 }}>
        <Button title="Add to Cart" style={{ flex: 1 }} />
        <Button 
          icon={<Share2 size={18} />} 
          variant="outline"
        />
      </View>
    </Card>
  )
}
```

### With Image

```tsx
import { Card, Image, Text } from 'prizmux'

<Card>
  <Image 
    source={{ uri: 'https://example.com/image.jpg' }}
    style={{ width: '100%', height: 200 }}
  />
  <View style={{ padding: 16 }}>
    <Text variant="title">Image Title</Text>
  </View>
</Card>
```

## Composition Patterns

Card works best when composed with other components:

```tsx
// Profile card
<Card>
  <ProfileHeader />
  <Divider />
  <ProfileStats />
  <Divider />
  <ProfileActions />
</Card>

// List item card
<Card onPress={() => navigate('detail')}>
  <ListItemContent />
</Card>

// Form card
<Card>
  <FormFields />
  <FormActions />
</Card>
```

## Notes

- Use Card as a container, not for complex layouts
- Combine padding prop with custom View styles for fine-grained control
- Pressable cards automatically show feedback on press
- Card respects theme colors automatically
- Perfect for implementing list items

document.title: Empty State
document.description: A component for displaying empty data states

body.raw:
# Empty State

EmptyState is a component that displays helpful messaging when there's no data to show. Perfect for guiding users when content is unavailable.

## Overview

EmptyState combines an icon, title, description, and optional action to create a complete empty state experience. It helps users understand why nothing is displayed and what to do next.

**Use it for:**
- No search results
- Empty lists or feeds
- First-time user experiences
- Data not available states
- Error fallbacks

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `icon` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Icon to display |
| `title` | `string` | - | Main message |
| `description` | `string` | - | Secondary message |
| `action` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Action button |
| `onAction` | `() => void` | - | Action callback |
| `variant` | `'default' \| 'compact'` | `'default'` | Layout variant |

## Usage

### Basic Empty State

```tsx
import { EmptyState } from 'prizmux'
import { Search } from 'lucide-react-native'

export default function SearchResults() {
  return (
    <EmptyState
      icon={<Search size={48} />}
      title="No Results"
      description="Try adjusting your search terms"
    />
  )
}
```

### With Action

```tsx
import { EmptyState, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { Plus } from 'lucide-react-native'

<EmptyState
  icon={<Plus size={48} />}
  title="No Items"
  description="Create your first item to get started"
  action={
    <Button 
      title="Create Item"
      onPress={() => navigation.navigate('Create')}
    />
  }
/>
```

### No Search Results

```tsx
import { EmptyState } from 'prizmux'
import { SearchX } from 'lucide-react-native'

function SearchList() {
  const [results, setResults] = useState([])
  const [query, setQuery] = useState('')

  return (
    <>
      <SearchBar value={query} onChange={setQuery} />
      {results.length === 0 ? (
        <EmptyState
          icon={<SearchX size={48} />}
          title="No Results"
          description={`No items found for "${query}"`}
        />
      ) : (
        <List data={results} />
      )}
    </>
  )
}
```

### Empty Feed

```tsx
import { EmptyState, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { Inbox } from 'lucide-react-native'

<EmptyState
  icon={<Inbox size={48} />}
  title="Your Feed is Empty"
  description="Follow users or add interests to see content"
  action={
    <Button
      title="Find Users to Follow"
      variant="primary"
      onPress={() => navigation.navigate('Discover')}
    />
  }
/>
```

### Error State

```tsx
import { EmptyState, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react-native'

function LoadingScreen() {
  const [error, setError] = useState(null)

  return error ? (
    <EmptyState
      icon={<AlertCircle size={48} />}
      title="Something Went Wrong"
      description={error.message}
      action={
        <Button 
          title="Try Again"
          onPress={() => retryLoad()}
        />
      }
    />
  ) : (
    <LoadingList />
  )
}
```

### Compact Variant

```tsx
<EmptyState
  icon={<Archive size={32} />}
  title="Archived"
  description="No archived items"
  variant="compact"
/>
```

### No Favorites

```tsx
import { EmptyState, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { Heart } from 'lucide-react-native'

function FavoritesScreen() {
  return (
    <EmptyState
      icon={<Heart size={48} />}
      title="No Favorites Yet"
      description="Items you favorite will appear here"
      action={
        <Button
          title="Browse Items"
          onPress={() => navigation.navigate('Explore')}
        />
      }
    />
  )
}
```

### Offline State

```tsx
import { EmptyState, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { WifiOff } from 'lucide-react-native'

<EmptyState
  icon={<WifiOff size={48} />}
  title="You're Offline"
  description="Check your internet connection"
  action={
    <Button
      title="Retry"
      onPress={() => refetch()}
    />
  }
/>
```

## Notes

- Use meaningful icons that relate to the empty state
- Keep titles concise and action-oriented
- Descriptions can provide helpful context
- Action button is optional but recommended
- Automatically scales on different screen sizes
- Works in both light and dark modes

document.title: Header with Back
document.description: A navigation header with back button and customization

body.raw:
# Header with Back

HeaderWithBack is a navigation component that provides a header with a back button, title, and action buttons. Perfect for screen transitions and navigation flows.

## Overview

HeaderWithBack handles standard navigation patterns while remaining flexible for custom needs. It automatically manages safe area insets and provides haptic feedback.

**Use it for:**
- Screen headers
- Navigation flows
- Detail screens
- Modal headers
- Settings screens

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `title` | `string` | - | Header title |
| `onBack` | `() => void` | - | Called when back button pressed |
| `subtitle` | `string` | - | Optional subtitle |
| `rightAction` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Right-side button/icon |
| `onRightAction` | `() => void` | - | Right action callback |
| `backIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | - | Custom back icon |
| `safeArea` | `boolean` | `true` | Respect safe area |

## Usage

### Basic Header

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack } from 'prizmux'

export default function DetailsScreen() {
  return (
    <>
      <HeaderWithBack
        title="Product Details"
        onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
      />
      {/* Screen content */}
    </>
  )
}
```

### With Subtitle

```tsx
<HeaderWithBack
  title="Messages"
  subtitle="John Doe"
  onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
/>
```

### With Action Button

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack } from 'prizmux'
import { Share2 } from 'lucide-react-native'

<HeaderWithBack
  title="Article"
  onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
  rightAction={<Share2 size={24} />}
  onRightAction={() => shareArticle()}
/>
```

### With Multiple Right Actions

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { MoreVertical } from 'lucide-react-native'
import { View } from 'react-native'

<HeaderWithBack
  title="Settings"
  onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
  rightAction={
    <View style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8 }}>
      <Button icon={<Edit size={24} />} variant="ghost" />
      <Button icon={<MoreVertical size={24} />} variant="ghost" />
    </View>
  }
/>
```

### Custom Back Icon

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack } from 'prizmux'
import { ChevronLeft } from 'lucide-react-native'

<HeaderWithBack
  title="Screen Title"
  backIcon={<ChevronLeft size={28} color="red" />}
  onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
/>
```

### In Navigation Stack

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack } from 'prizmux'
import { useFocusEffect } from '@react-navigation/native'

export default function ProfileScreen({ navigation }) {
  useFocusEffect(() => {
    navigation.setOptions({
      header: () => (
        <HeaderWithBack
          title="Profile"
          onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
          rightAction={<Edit size={24} />}
          onRightAction={() => navigation.push('EditProfile')}
        />
      ),
    })
  })

  return (
    // Screen content
  )
}
```

### Modal Header

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack } from 'prizmux'
import { X } from 'lucide-react-native'

<HeaderWithBack
  title="Filter Options"
  backIcon={<X size={24} />}
  onBack={() => dismissModal()}
/>
```

### With Loading State

```tsx
import { HeaderWithBack, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { Save } from 'lucide-react-native'
import { useState } from 'react'

export default function EditScreen() {
  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false)

  const handleSave = async () => {
    setSaving(true)
    await saveChanges()
    setSaving(false)
  }

  return (
    <>
      <HeaderWithBack
        title="Edit Profile"
        onBack={() => navigation.goBack()}
        rightAction={
          <Button
            icon={<Save size={20} />}
            loading={saving}
            variant="ghost"
            onPress={handleSave}
          />
        }
      />
    </>
  )
}
```

## Notes

- Automatically handles safe area insets on notched devices
- Back button provides haptic feedback on press
- Right action can be any component (Button, Icon, custom)
- Title is automatically truncated if too long
- Subtitle appears below title in smaller text
- Respects system back gesture on iOS and Android

document.title: Image Preview
document.description: A gallery component with zoom and swipe gestures

body.raw:
# Image Preview

ImagePreview is a gallery component that displays images with pinch-to-zoom, swipe-to-navigate, and smooth animations.

## Overview

ImagePreview provides a full-featured image viewing experience with gesture support. Perfect for displaying photos, product images, and media galleries.

**Use it for:**
- Photo galleries
- Product image viewers
- Media albums
- Document previews
- Portfolio displays

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `images` | `ImageSource[]` | - | Array of images to display |
| `initialIndex` | `number` | `0` | Starting image index |
| `onIndexChange` | `(index: number) => void` | - | Called when swiping |
| `allowZoom` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable pinch-to-zoom |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | - | Called when closing |
| `showIndicator` | `boolean` | `true` | Show page indicator |

## Usage

### Basic Gallery

```tsx
import { ImagePreview } from 'prizmux'

export default function App() {
  const images = [
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo1.jpg' },
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo2.jpg' },
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo3.jpg' },
  ]

  return <ImagePreview images={images} />
}
```

### With Initial Index

```tsx
<ImagePreview 
  images={productImages}
  initialIndex={2}
/>
```

### Controlled Gallery

```tsx
import { ImagePreview, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { useState } from 'react'
import { View } from 'react-native'

export default function Gallery() {
  const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(0)

  const images = [
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo1.jpg' },
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo2.jpg' },
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo3.jpg' },
  ]

  return (
    <View style={{ flex: 1 }}>
      <ImagePreview
        images={images}
        initialIndex={currentIndex}
        onIndexChange={setCurrentIndex}
      />
      
      <View style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8 }}>
        <Button 
          title="Previous"
          disabled={currentIndex === 0}
          onPress={() => setCurrentIndex(currentIndex - 1)}
        />
        <Button 
          title="Next"
          disabled={currentIndex === images.length - 1}
          onPress={() => setCurrentIndex(currentIndex + 1)}
        />
      </View>
    </View>
  )
}
```

### Modal Gallery

```tsx
import { ImagePreview, Button } from 'prizmux'
import { useState } from 'react'
import { View, Modal } from 'react-native'

export default function ModalGallery() {
  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false)
  
  const images = [
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo1.jpg' },
    { uri: 'https://example.com/photo2.jpg' },
  ]

  return (
    <>
      <Button 
        title="View Gallery"
        onPress={() => setVisible(true)}
      />
      
      <Modal
        visible={visible}
        animationType="fade"
      >
        <View style={{ flex: 1, backgroundColor: '#000' }}>
          <ImagePreview
            images={images}
            onClose={() => setVisible(false)}
          />
        </View>
      </Modal>
    </>
  )
}
```

### Product Gallery

```tsx
import { ImagePreview, Card, Text } from 'prizmux'
import { useState } from 'react'
import { View, Image, TouchableOpacity } from 'react-native'

export default function ProductGallery() {
  const [selectedIndex, setSelectedIndex] = useState(0)
  
  const images = product.images.map(url => ({ uri: url }))

  return (
    <View>
      <ImagePreview
        images={images}
        initialIndex={selectedIndex}
        onIndexChange={setSelectedIndex}
      />
      
      <View style={{ flexDirection: 'row', gap: 8, marginTop: 12 }}>
        {images.map((image, index) => (
          <TouchableOpacity
            key={index}
            onPress={() => setSelectedIndex(index)}
            style={{ 
              borderWidth: index === selectedIndex ? 2 : 0,
              borderRadius: 8,
              overflow: 'hidden'
            }}
          >
            <Image
              source={image}
              style={{ width: 60, height: 60 }}
            />
          </TouchableOpacity>
        ))}
      </View>
    </View>
  )
}
```

## Gestures

- **Swipe left/right** — Navigate between images
- **Pinch** — Zoom in/out (when enabled)
- **Double tap** — Auto-zoom to content
- **Pan while zoomed** — Move around zoomed image

## Notes

- Images are cached for smooth scrolling
- Zoom resets when swiping to next image
- Supports both local and remote images
- Page indicator shows current position
- Optimized for performance with large galleries
